response.original_title: Stonehenge - Panorama of the Ancient Druidical Remains
response.overview: This is one of hundreds of panoramas made in the early days of film, when views of tourist attractions - like the ancient British icon, Stonehenge - became increasingly prolific in various media, as Victorian leisure time increased. In this view a policeman stands next to the great stones as the camera pans past, to give an impression of scale.
response.title: Stonehenge - Panorama of the Ancient Druidical Remains
